Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the user has provided a detailed description of a multinational smartphone supply chain and wants 20 open ended questions to explore it further

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key here is to generate thought provoking questions that cover various aspects like efficiency risks sustainability and innovation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process spans RD sourcing manufacturing logistics distribution and after sales support Each stage has its own complexities and interdependencies

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The questions should dig into potential bottlenecks strategic trade offs and emerging trends

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I can structure the questions to address different layers of the supply chain

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example starting with broader strategic questions about globalization and agility then moving to specific operational areas like supplier management manufacturing resilience and logistics

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should also touch on sustainability technology adoption and customer centric aspects

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Its important to avoid overly technical or narrow questions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Instead focus on open ended prompts that encourage deep analysis and creative thinking

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The goal is to help the user uncover insights they might not have considered like the impact of geopolitical tensions on sourcing or how circular economy principles could be applied

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should flow logically from upstream to downstream processes while weaving in cross cutting themes like digital transformation and risk management

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This way the user gets a comprehensive set of questions to explore the supply chain holistically
